Visit_1.input_text:
=== FIELD: History of Presenting Illness ===
Collateral history from the grandmother: 6 year old female with Cerebral Palsy following perinatal asphyxia brought in by the grandmother, been attending neuroclinic from Nsambya Hospital- however not in possession of any medical documents and mother not able to provide comprehensive history. She is however requesting for transfer of care to mulago neuroclinic due to financial constraints; currently child is on sodium valproate (dose unknown) and has previously been on phenobarbital following generalised convulsions 2 days ago and also Tegretol.  Initially followed up from Butabika National Referral and got lost to follow up    O/E Fair general condition, afebrile, no pallor, no jaundice, no dehydration    R/S: Not in obvious distress, equal air entry bilaterally with vesicular breath sounds.    CVS: Warm peripheries, CRT< 2 seconds, HS I + II no added sounds.    P/A: Normal fullness, soft, no organomegalies/masses

=== FIELD: Current drug regimen ===
Sodium Valproate, Phenobarbital, Tegretol (Carbamazepine)
Visit_1.output_text: 02/01/2022 (Newly Prescribed Medication) 1. Continue Na Valproate 100 mg morning × 1/52 2. PO Folate 5 mg od × 2/52 3. Tabs Baclofen 5 mg tds × 1/12

output_columns.Current dose: Na Valproate 100 mg morning × 1/52, PO Folate 5 mg od × 2/52, Tabs Baclofen 5 mg tds × 1/12

Visit_2.input_text:
=== FIELD: Second Entry(6 months) ===
6 year old female being managed for CP with Epilepsy on Sodium Valproate, Baclofen and physiotherapy. Daddy reports that she has one episode of seizure attack. Duration: few seconds. No special concerns today.    O/E CNS: fully conscious, generalised hypertonia in all limbs. Rt > Lt

output_columns.Medication dosage and if there was a cha: 1. Sodium Valproate 150 mg BD × 3/12, 2. Folic Acid 5 mg OD ×3/12, 3. Baclofen 5 mg tds × 3/12

Visit_3.input_text:
=== FIELD: Third Entry(12 months) ===
6 6/12 old patient with cerebral palsy and epilepsy, currently on sodium valproate at 20mg/ kg/day; baclofen and physiotherapy. Father reports that child gets seizures involving the lips.    O/E: FGC well nourished with no pallor, no jaundice, no edema, afebrile, poor dental hygiene with dental caries.    CNS: Conscious with abnormal movements. Pupils equal and reactive to light. Neck soft with generalised hypertonia and hyper-reflexia.

output_columns.Medication dosage and if there was a cha: 1. Tabs Baclofen 5 mg bd × 3/12, 2. Tabs Sodium Valproate 150 mg bd × 3/12

Visit_1.input_text:
=== FIELD: History of Presenting Illness ===
1-year 10/12 month old with no known chronic illnesses, was well till 4/12 ago when he developed convulsions that started with the right upper and lower limbs that later progressed to involve the whole body. The child has had convulsions 4 times since August 2022 (last one was yesterday) and has not been on anticonvulsant medication till yesterday when he was started on phenytoin syrup 5 mls bd. Mother reports that she started ANC at 3 months and attended monthly ujntil she delivered. Pregnancy was uneventful. Delivered by SVD at term BWT 4.3 kg, neonatal period was uneventful. No history of any febrile illnesses. There is history of seizure disorder in the family (maternal grandfather)    O/E: FGC, very irritable with excessive crying, no pallor, no jaundice, no dehydration, no edema.    CNS: Very irritable, fully conscious, normal tone in all limbs, no FNDs    R/S: Not in distress, Equal bilateral air entry, bronchovesicular breath sounds.    CVS: Warm extremities, CRT < 2 sec, HS I + II + 0    P/A: Normal Fullness, soft and no palpable masses.

input_columns.Detailed description of seizure history: Was well till 4/12 ago when he developed convulsions that started with the right upper and lower limbs that later progressed to involve the whole body. The child has had convulsions 4 times since August 2022 (last one was yesterday)

Visit_2.input_text:
=== FIELD: Second Entry(6 months) ===
2 1/2 year old male with Epilepsy on sodium valproate seizure onset August 2022. Medication began January 2023, since medication began he had one episode of seizure attack (duration: 20 min)

Visit_3.input_text:
=== FIELD: Third Entry(12 months) ===
Reviewed a 3-year-old male with epilepsy on Sodium Valproate at 18 mg/ kg/ day. Mother reports seizures are frequent and not well controlled.    O/E: FGC, Afebrile, Not pale, No Jaundice    CNS: Fully alert, Normal tone and No FNDs

output_columns.Medication dosage and if there was a cha: 1. Increase Na Valproate to 25 mg/kg/day. Tabs Sodium Valproate 200mg BD × 2/12 2. Tabs Levetiracetam 250 mg B.D  × 2/12

Visit_1.input_text:
=== FIELD: History of Presenting Illness ===
History of seizures on and off, periodically for the past 7 months. Noted history and findings suggestive of Epilepsy R/O ADHD

input_columns.Detailed description of seizure history: History of seizures on and off, periodically for the past 7 months. Noted history and findings suggestive of Epilepsy R/O ADHD

Visit_2.input_text:
=== FIELD: Second Entry(6 months) ===
5-year-old male referred from MAC Paediatrics with a history of period seizures × 7/12. Child has been brought by one of the care givers at home. Care giver reports that the child gets GTC seizures atleast every moth, exacerbated with an illness. When an episode comes, it lasts about 5-10 minutes with post-related lethargy and, the child loses consciousness and produces form at the mouth but no fecal or urinary incontinence. The episodes are reduced with carbamazepine    ROS - Unremarkable    Natal/Immunisation/Nutrition: Care giver is not.    Family Social History: Stays with grandmother. Parents are away and unreachable. Not in school due to illness    O/E: Fair general condition with no pallor, no jaundice, no dehydration, responds well, afebrile. No wounds on skin    MSK: Has normal joint and muscle movement.    CVS: S1 and S2 Heard and Normal    P/A: Soft, no palpable organomegaly    CNS: Active GCS 15/15, PEARL, Normal joint, Normal tone, power and reflexes. No cranial nerve palsy    Impression: ? Epileptic Seizures
Visit_2.output_text: Plan 1. Do EEG  2. Tabs Albendazole 400 mg stat  3. Tabs Carbamazepine 200 mg bd × 1/12  4. Tabs Folic Acid 5 mg od × 1/12  5. Review with results of EEG

output_columns.Medication dosage and if there was a cha: 05-01-2023: Tabs Albendazole 400 mg stat, Tabs Carbamazepine 200 mg bd × 1/12, Tabs Folic Acid 5 mg od × 1/12. Notable change since 8/11/2023. Patient was previous on Tabs Carbamazepine 100 mg bd ×1/12 increased to Tabs Carbamazepine 200 mg bd × 1/12 and Tabs Folic Acid 5 mg od × 1/12

Visit_3.input_text: 5/M who is being managed for epilepsy, last had a convulsion in February 2023. EEG- Abnormal EEG. O/E Good general condition, no pallor, no jaundice and no edema Neuro examination un-remarkable Imp: Generalised Epilepsy

output_columns.Medication dosage and if there was a cha: Tabs Sodium Valproate 150 mg bd × 2/52 Then 200 mg bd ×2/12

Visit_1.input_text:
=== FIELD: History of Presenting Illness ===
1. Seizures × 7/12  She was well till 7/12 ago when she developed a high fever which was treated as Malaria. Mother reported later the child started falling down and hitting her head on the ground about 6 episodes ground for about 2 minutes. No LOC  ROS - Unremarkable    Past Medical History: She has had several admissions in peripheral clinics ever since her development. Currently on Carbamazepine and Phenobarbitone    Past Surgical History: Unremarkable    Perinatal history: Uneventful pregnancy. Delivered at term. Birth weight: 3.0 kg, cried immediately. No neonatal illness.    Imp: Myoclonic Atonic Epilepsy (Doose Syndrome)    Brain CT Scan: Normal brain Morphology  No SOL    
=== FIELD: Current drug regimen ===
Previously on Carbamazepine (unknown dose)
Visit_1.output_text:
Plan 1. Do EEG  2. Stop the carbamazepine  3. Tabs Ethosuximide 250 mg o.d × 2/52
05/01/2022: Carbamazepine stopped and started on Tabs Ethosuximide 250 mg od × 2/52

input_columns.Detailed description of seizure history: Seizures × 7/12  She was well till 7/12 ago when she developed a high fever which was treated as Malaria. Mother reported later the child started falling down and hitting her head on the ground about 6 episodes ground for about 2 minutes. No LOC

Visit_2.input_text:
=== FIELD: Second Entry(6 months) ===
4/F being managed for Doose Syndrome on Ethoxusamide. Mother reports the seizures (drop attacks) have gradually reduced and currently has had no seizure in the last week.    EEG-Normal background pattern that is grossly punctuated by generalised epileptiform hypersynchonies  DDX: Doose Syndrome    O/E: Asleep with healing wounds on the frontal region
Visit_2.output_text: Plan 1. Tabs Ethoxusamide 250 mg od × 1/12  2. Advised on helmet

Visit_3.input_text: Reviewed a 4/F being managed for Doose Syndrome on Ethoxusimide and Sodium Valproate, has been seizure free since the last visit (23/3/2024) O/E FGC, afebrile, no pallor, no jaundice, no dehydration, no edema CNS: No muscle wasting, no skin lesions, normal tone and reflexes RS: Not in distress Imp: A 4 /F with Doose Syndrome
Visit_3.output_text: Plan: 1. Continue Ethoxusimide 250 mg o.d × 3/12   2. Tabs Sodium Valproate 200 mg bd × 3/12  3. To be reviewed in Soroti since it is closer.

output_columns.Medication dosage and if there was a cha: Continue Ethoxusimide 250 mg o.d × 3/12  2. Tabs Sodium Valproate 200 mg bd × 3/12. NB Sodium Valproate was commenced on the23/03/2024

Visit_1.input_text:
=== FIELD: History of Presenting Illness ===
1. Seizure since birth  Known CP patient who presented with tonic seizures since birth. Mother reports they have increased in intensity and frequency. In the last week the seizures have been controlled on carbamazepine. Mother reports about 3 episodes in a day and they last about 1 minute.  Perinatal History. The pregnancy was uneventful, she had prolonged and obstructed then had an emergency c/s. The baby didn't cry immediately and was admitted in NICU × 14/7 and had convulsions during the time.    Growth & Motor: Cannot sit without help    Family Social History: First born to the mother. Stays with both parents.    O/E Fair General Condition, microcephaly, afebrile, no pallor, no jaundice    R/S RR-26 bpm, Equal air entry bilaterally with bronchovesicular breath sounds.    CNS: Has generalised hypertonia with hyper reflexia. No contractions. Microcephaly    Imp: 2-year 2/12 months old female with spastic CP. Tonic Seizures

=== FIELD: Current drug regimen ===
Tabs Carbamazepine 75 mg bd × 2/12
Visit_1.output_text: Plan 1. Do EEG  2. Tabs Carbamazepine 75 mg bd × 2/12

input_columns.Detailed description of seizure history: Known CP patient who presented with tonic seizures since birth. Mother reports they have increased in intensity and frequency. In the last week the seizures have been controlled on carbamazepine. Mother reports about 3 episodes in a day and they last about 1 minute.
input_columns.Duration of Seizure: 3 episodes in a day and they last about 1 minute
